Details: The effectiveness of GAC for the treatment of arsenic has been assessed in various bench-, pilot-, and full-scale studies. In many of the studies, the GAC was modified by the incorporation of constituents such as organic chlorides [2694], organic bromides [2694], iron [2697, 2699, 2707, 2713], or manganese [2707]. Effectiveness was found to be highly variable, with bench-scale studies ranging from 72 to 98 percent removal [2694, 2699, 2707, 2710, 2713], pilot-scale studies ranging from 11 to 98 percent removal [604, 2717], and a full-scale study ranging from 0 to 95 percent [2689]. The treatment conditions and specific GAC chosen will ultimately determine the feasibility for GAC as a treatment for arsenic.
